blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: How's your week been since Thursday?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Better. Calmer.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Did you talk to anyone about it? About Daniel?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Mr. Cole talked to me in the car. He said some things that — I don't know. Helped me get it in order.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: What kind of things?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Like — I already know what to do. I just have to trust it when things are actually happening. Not think my way out of it after.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: That's hard.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. It is.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I think you do though. Trust yourself. More than you think you do.
I looked at her.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Because you didn't fight him. There were three of them and you didn't swing. That's not weakness. That's knowing something.
The previews started. She looked back at the screen but I stayed looking at her for a second too long.
Friday night. Jonathan on AIM.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: what'd you do today

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: nothing really. hung with Diego for a bit.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: that's it?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: yeah.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: ...okay
He knew something was off. I could feel it through the screen. He wasn't going to ask directly — that wasn't how Jonathan operated. He'd just let the silence sit until I said something.
I didn't say anything. Friday turned into Saturday without anything happening that was worth saying.

blocks.prompt: Crystal says you trust yourself more than you think. The previews started.

options.label: Say nothing. Hold it. Look back at the screen.
You look at her a beat too long. She notices.
